title: Space Force weighs launch alternatives as Vulcan faces potential months-long grounding
summary: The U.S. Space Force is considering alternative launch options due to potential months-long grounding of the Vulcan rocket. Officials are exploring shifting missions and extending the lifespan of existing satellites.

title: Office of Space Commerce releases mission authorization proposal
summary: The Office of Space Commerce has proposed a 'light touch' approach to mission authorization to regulate new commercial space applications. This is the latest effort to establish guidelines for commercial space activities.

title: Terran Orbital Introduces New Star Tracker Product Line at SATSHOW 2026
summary: Terran Orbital Corporation, a Lockheed Martin company, unveiled a new star tracker product line at SATSHOW 2026, expanding its offerings in space technology. This marks a significant addition to the company's portfolio in the satellite and space navigation sector.

title: Telesat eyes defense role with laser comms test ahead of LEO debut
summary: Telesat is expanding into defense applications by testing laser communications ahead of its planned low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite deployment. The company aims to demonstrate military Ka-band capabilities and plans a 2027 NASA demonstration as the Pentagon seeks faster space data transport.

title: Sift raises $42 million in Series B round
summary: Sift, a Southern California startup, raised $42 million in a Series B funding round to expand its engineering team and infrastructure for AI-controlled devices. The company focuses on tools to help engineers interpret hardware sensor data.

title: Infinity Avionics Unveils Aquila: The Next-Generation Space Imaging Solution for Complex Orbital Operations
summary: Infinity Avionics launched Aquila, a next-generation space imaging solution for complex orbital operations, after its debut at the IAC Sydney. The commercial availability of Aquila marks a significant advancement in smart vision systems for the NewSpace economy.

title: Airbase raises $5 million to tackle spectrum bottleneck as FCC eyes new space uses
summary: Airbase, a New York-based software startup, raised $5 million to address spectrum coordination challenges for satellites and wireless systems. The funding comes as the FCC explores new uses for space.

title: Musk wants to go to the moon. But how will he build his ‘self-growing city’?
summary: Elon Musk has expressed ambitions to establish a self-sustaining city on the Moon, though his claims are often met with skepticism due to past unfulfilled promises. The article explores the feasibility of such a project and the challenges involved.

title: Pave Space raises $40 million to develop European heavy kickstage
summary: Swiss startup Pave Space secured $40 million in funding to develop an orbital transfer vehicle designed to move satellites from Low Earth Orbit (LEO) to their final destinations in hours instead of months. This innovation aims to significantly reduce satellite deployment timelines in the European space industry.

title: NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations
summary: NASA is revising its approach to fostering commercial space stations to replace the ISS, citing slow progress in market development. The new strategy aims to accelerate the transition to private orbital outposts.

title: Inside Artemis 2: NASA's historic astronaut moon mission explained (video series)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission is explained in a four-part video series by Space.com, debuting from March 25 to March 31. This mission marks a historic step in NASA's Artemis program aimed at returning astronauts to the Moon.

title: 100 new alien worlds: Scientists find hidden haul in data from NASA exoplanet-hunting spacecraft
summary: Astronomers discovered 100 new exoplanets using data from NASA's TESS spacecraft and an AI program. This expands the known catalog of alien worlds beyond our solar system.

title: Jupiter and the moon take a sunset stroll on March 26. Here's how to see it
summary: Jupiter and the Moon will appear close together in the sky at sunset on March 26. This conjunction can be observed with the naked eye.

title: Canada cancels its 1st moon rover: 'It's hopefully not a lost cause'
summary: Canada has canceled its first-ever moon rover mission, which was planned to land near the lunar south pole in 2029. The cancellation has sparked concerns about the future of lunar exploration in the country.

title: Get $28 off this Lego Star Wars Dark Falcon set or suffer at the hands of Darth Jar Jar
summary: A Lego Star Wars Dark Falcon set is being offered at a 16% discount during Amazon's Big Spring Sale, featuring exclusive minifigures and a Sith-themed Han Solo ship. The promotion coincides with the release of Disney+'s Lego Star Wars: Rebuild the Galaxy.

title: We've finally seen 'Exodus' gameplay, and it's 'Mass Effect' with a space grappling hook
summary: Gameplay footage for 'Exodus' has been revealed, showcasing space combat mechanics reminiscent of 'Mass Effect' with a unique space grappling hook feature.

title: A photographer's journey to capture a blood moon rising over the South China Sea. 'It was an incredible moment'
summary: A photographer captured a blood moon rising over the South China Sea, describing the moment as incredible and instinctively overwhelming. The event highlights the beauty of natural celestial phenomena.

title: Rocket shines under the northern lights | Space photo of the day for March 25, 2026
summary: A stunning photo captures the northern lights illuminating the night sky above Isar Aerospace's Spectrum rocket. The image was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for March 25, 2026.

title: Like 'accelerating from stationary to supersonic flight': Europe's Hera probe boosts speed, stays on course for November asteroid rendezvous
summary: Europe's Hera probe has successfully completed a deep-space maneuver, maintaining its trajectory for a November rendezvous with the asteroid system targeted by NASA's DART mission in 2022.

title: Europe plans EPIC SpaceX mission to send 4 astronauts to the ISS before its retirement
summary: The European Space Agency is planning to purchase a dedicated SpaceX Crew Dragon mission to send four astronauts to the International Space Station before its retirement. This mission aims to enhance Europe's astronaut time and experience in space.

title: La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la misión lunar Artemis II
summary: NASA announces continuous coverage updates for the Artemis II lunar mission, including pre-launch, launch, and mission operations events.

title: NASA Ames Experts Available for Artemis II Flight Test Interviews
summary: NASA's Ames Research Center is offering media interviews with experts ahead of the Artemis II flight test, which will send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in over 50 years. The interviews are scheduled for March 27 from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for First Artemis Crewed Mission Around Moon
summary: NASA has announced coverage details for the Artemis II mission, the first crewed flight around the Moon, targeting a launch window starting April 1. The mission marks a significant step in NASA's Artemis program.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Artemis II Moon Mission
summary: NASA will provide live coverage for the Artemis II crewed Moon mission, with launch targeted for April at 6:24 p.m. EDT. The agency will update briefings and mission events throughout prelaunch, launch, and mission phases.

title: Artemis Moon Tree Dedicated in Honor of Mary W. Jackson
summary: NASA dedicated an Artemis Moon Tree at Mary W. Jackson Elementary School in March 2026, honoring the legacy of Mary W. Jackson. The loblolly pine sapling was planted months prior and symbolizes the agency's Artemis program.

title: NASA Names Jennifer Lyons Acting Launch Services Program Manager
summary: NASA has appointed Jennifer Lyons as the acting program manager for the Launch Services Program (LSP) at Kennedy Space Center. She will oversee the acquisition and management of commercial launch services for science and robotic missions starting April 1.

title: Metrics
summary: NASA has released its FY25 Services Catalog and Service Level Agreement (SLA) outlining service descriptions, chargeback rates, and service level indicators for NSSC services.

title: Webb Captures Saturn in Infrared
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured an infrared image of Saturn's icy rings, which were released alongside a Hubble Space Telescope image on March 25, 2026. The combined data provides scientists with a more detailed understanding of the gas giant's atmosphere.

title: NASA Webb, Hubble Share Most Comprehensive View of Saturn to Date
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope and Hubble Space Telescope have collaborated to capture comprehensive new images of Saturn, offering contrasting views that enhance scientists' understanding of the planet's atmosphere. The observatories observed Saturn in complementary wavelengths, revealing layered details of the gas giant.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for 2028, with Administrator Jared Isaacman and agency leaders detailing the initiative.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on launch pads at Starbase in preparation for the upcoming Block 3 Starships, aiming to increase launch frequency. Engineers are actively working on ground infrastructure to support the new vehicle design.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the United States will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from Baikonur Cosmodrome's repaired Site 31/6. The mission follows repairs to the launch pad after a previous launch.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has revealed plans for an orbital data center. This marks a significant expansion in the company's space infrastructure ambitions.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the planned lunar mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. The development brings the next launch closer to reality.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is scheduled with nine expected missions, including launches by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly-induced blackout. The mission team confirmed the recovery, marking a significant milestone for the mission.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting a series of industry events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025. These events will outline planned activities, thematic priorities, and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use for financial proposals in ESA tender actions. The new software aims to standardize costing processes for ESA suppliers.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the root cause and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have initiated experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic and human movement. This initiative aims to advance space technology and educational research.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce proposed a one-stop licensing system for novel space missions to streamline regulatory authority amid ongoing debates over mission authorization frameworks. This initiative aims to address gaps in existing regulations for unconventional space activities.

title: PAVE Space Raises $40M for In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: PAVE Space has secured $40 million in funding to advance its in-space propulsion technology, which uses a storable bipropellant to simplify and reduce the cost of launch operations compared to cryogenic fuels.

title: Airbase Emerges From Stealth With $5M to Automate Spectrum Allocation
summary: Airbase, a new startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $5 million in funding to automate spectrum allocation for the growing number of space missions. The company aims to address the increasing demand for spectrum management as orbital activities expand.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman's vision for a commercially driven lunar economy has evolved, now including a detailed plan. The updated priorities emphasize surface infrastructure to support this goal.

title: One-On-One With SDA’s GP Sandhoo
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) launched its first operational tranche of satellites six months ago, but troops are not yet benefiting due to minor issues forcing a 'strategic pause'. SDA Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo discussed these challenges in an interview with Payload.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio Hosted Payload Platform
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio hosted payload platform, enabling payloads to be attached to and detached from satellites in orbit. This model aims to revolutionize satellite operations by allowing flexible payload hosting.

title: Ramon.Space, Ingrasys Aim To Fly Prototype Orbital Data Center In 2027
summary: Ramon.Space and Ingrasys plan to launch a prototype orbital data center in 2027, joining the growing space-based data center industry.

title: Beyond the Rocket: The Digital Infrastructure of the Artemis II Mission
summary: The Artemis II mission relies on advanced digital infrastructure unseen by the public, marking a significant technological leap from Apollo. NASA's digital architecture ensures mission success, as highlighted by Booz Allen's Karen Fields.

title: Contrivian Launches New Product Bundling Amazon Leo & Starlink
summary: Contrivian has launched a new product that bundles satellite connectivity services from Amazon's Project Kuiper (Leo) and SpaceX's Starlink, eliminating the need for customers to choose between providers. This initiative aims to offer more flexible and comprehensive satellite internet solutions.

title: SpaceX v. Amazon Heats Up at the FCC
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are engaged in a regulatory dispute at the FCC regarding their satellite constellations. The public filings reveal a contentious and dramatic exchange between the two companies.

title: Interview With Kepler Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis at SATShow Week
summary: Kepler Communications' Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis discusses the company's strategy for downlinking data from space to Earth during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Kepler's approach to addressing this key challenge in the space industry.

title: The Evolving Influence of IFC Driving Network Development
summary: Airlines have adopted inflight connectivity (IFC) as a standard offering for passengers over the past two decades. IFC developers are now focusing on advancing network capabilities to meet growing demand.

title: Government Investment, Regulations Are the Key Trends to Watch in India, APAC Space
summary: Government investment and regulations are expected to drive the growth of the space economy in India and the Asia Pacific region. The role of governments as investors and market creators will be a dominant trend.

title: Interview With Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski at SATShow Week
summary: Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski discusses the first delivery of Comtech’s Digital Common Ground 7000 series modems at SATShow Week, featuring live multi-orbit demonstrations.

title: Space Commerce Official Proposes Streamlined Mission Authorization Process for Novel Space Missions
summary: U.S. Office of Space Commerce Director Taylor Jordan proposed a streamlined mission authorization process for novel space activities during SATShow Week. The initiative aims to simplify regulations for emerging space missions.

title: Inside the Data Fusion and Integration Challenges to Building the Golden Dome
summary: The article discusses the data fusion and integration challenges involved in building the Golden Dome missile defense system, which tracks and intercepts ballistic or hypersonic missiles using a 3D tracking system.

title: Geopolitical Shakeups and Capital Wars Push Top Operators to Iterate Faster
summary: Geopolitical conflicts and rising concerns are highlighting the critical role of satellite communications, pushing top operators to accelerate innovation. Commercial satellite leaders emphasize the need for faster iteration in response to these developments.

title: Launchers Cite Busy Manifests and Scarcity as Commercial Demand Grows
summary: Launch providers report extremely busy manifests for the next two years due to growing commercial demand. SpaceX is highlighted as a key player in the heavy-lift launch competition.

title: Lynk and SES CEOs Talk D2D Demand, As D2D Merger Awaits Completion
summary: Lynk and SES CEOs discuss the growing demand for direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity amid market competition and geopolitical challenges. The merger of the two companies in this sector is still pending completion.

title: SDA Takes ‘Strategic Pause’ on Launches to Work Through Fixes
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) has paused launches to address and implement necessary fixes. This strategic pause aims to improve satellite production and acquisition processes.
